activities_projects.title: Factors Affecting Agriculture
activities_projects.objective: Understand the physical and human factors influencing agriculture
steps: Teacher explains factors such as climate, soil, and technology
Students list physical and human factors affecting farming
Create two columns and classify each factor
Discuss how each factor influences crop production
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the major factors that influence agriculture
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and classification of factors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Subsistence and Commercial Farming
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the differences between farming types
steps: Divide the chart paper into two sections
List features of subsistence farming
List features of commercial farming
Compare inputs, output, and purpose of production
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students differentiate between subsistence and commercial farming
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Crop Season Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between seasons and crop cultivation
steps: Teacher explains Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid seasons
Students list crops grown in each season
Analyze why certain crops grow better in specific seasons
Write a short explanation in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand seasonal patterns of agriculture
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Map Work: Major Crop Regions of India
activities_projects.objective: Locate important agricultural regions in India
steps: Mark regions where rice, wheat, tea, and cotton are produced
Label the states clearly
Use atlas reference if needed
Discuss climatic reasons for these crop patterns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand spatial distribution of crops in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and neatness of map work (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Irrigation Methods Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn about traditional and modern irrigation methods
steps: Teacher explains irrigation techniques such as canals, wells, and drip irrigation
Students list advantages and disadvantages of each method
Discuss the importance of irrigation in agriculture
Write a short summary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of irrigation in crop production
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of notes and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Tea Plantation
activities_projects.objective: Study plantation agriculture through an example
steps: Read about tea plantations in India
Identify regions where tea is grown
List conditions required for tea cultivation
Write a short case study
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand plantation agriculture and its requirements
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and completeness of case study (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Agricultural Tools Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Identify traditional and modern farming tools
steps: Teacher shows pictures of traditional and modern tools
Students identify each tool
Discuss differences between traditional and modern tools
Write their uses in agriculture
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how technology improves agricultural productivity

activities_projects.title: Food Crop vs Cash Crop Study
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between food crops and cash crops
steps: Students list examples of food crops such as rice and wheat
List examples of cash crops like cotton and tea
Compare their uses and importance
Discuss how they affect the economy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand economic importance of different crops
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of classification and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate on Organic vs Chemical Farming
steps: Divide students into two groups
One group supports organic farming
Another group supports chemical farming
Conduct a classroom debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze advantages and disadvantages of farming methods
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Project: Agricultural Practices in Our Region
steps: Collect information about crops grown locally
Interview a farmer or elder about farming practices
Note irrigation methods and tools used
Prepare a short report and present in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand real-life agricultural practices in their locality
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and presentation (10 marks)
